topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an oppressive intensity, mirroring the thrumming anxiety in Elara’s chest. The accusation, whispered in the hushed tones of the department meeting, had settled over her like a heavy, wet blanket. She traced the condensation ring left by her lukewarm coffee on the desk, her fingers trembling slightly. Every word she had ever written, every late night spent wrestling with complex theories, felt suddenly exposed, judged. When Professor Albright finally looked up, his gaze was flat, and the silence that followed was deafening, amplifying the frantic drumming in her ears. Elara could only stare at the polished wood grain of the table, wishing the floor would simply swallow her whole.

[story 2]

"I... I don't understand," Marcus mumbled, the words catching in his throat like shards of glass. He kept running a hand over his face, the skin feeling clammy beneath his touch. The accusation hung in the air, sharp and undeniable. He looked around the lecture hall, the sea of faces suddenly feeling like a hostile crowd, each pair of eyes seeming to bore into him. He felt a distinct tightness across his jaw, a physical constriction that made breathing shallow. He gripped the edge of his notebook so tightly his knuckles turned white, the paper beneath his fingers suddenly irrelevant. He wanted to disappear into the uncomfortable plastic chair.

[story 3]

The email arrived at precisely 2:00 PM. It was stark, formal, and devastating. Liam read the subject line three times before his eyes finally tracked down the body of the message. A coldness seeped into his bones, a profound chill that had nothing to do with the air conditioning. He stood by the window, watching the rain streak down the glass, each drop a tiny, relentless impact. His hands, resting on the sill, began to shake, a fine, uncontrollable tremor that made steadying his gaze impossible. He felt a strange detachment, as if he were observing the scene from a great distance, watching himself be dismantled piece by piece.
